name: SkillsBench-style paired comparison
research_question: When the same coding agent receives the same product brief, does Lamina (skills installed + project workflow conventions) produce better implemented product behavior than no Lamina skills and no workflow hint?

runner.description: One continuous agent rollout per trial via harbor run. No harness-forced slash-command phases.

control.label: No Lamina skills
control.description: Fixture + instruction.md only. No Lamina skills, no AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md workflow hint.

treatment.label: Lamina skills + workflow hint
treatment.description: Same fixture and instruction.md. Lamina skills in agent skill path plus AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md prescribing init → design → implement → verify → fix.

unattended_policy.prevention: Harbor prompt_template.j2 and treatment AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md include unattended contract: brief is authoritative; no clarify-and-STOP.
unattended_policy.on_stall: Verifier reward 0; clarify_stall logged in reward output (not in composite).

fairness_constraints: Same instruction.md product brief for both arms (no Lamina skill names in instructions — SkillsBench rule).
Same coding agent, model pin, temperature, task, fixture, and Harbor prompt_template wrapper per paired trial.
Control receives no Lamina skills and no workflow AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md.
Both arms score the same artifact type: bundled application source (.lamina/ and bench process files excluded).
Workflow hint lives only in treatment project conventions, not in task instructions.
explicit_non_claims: Does not claim the agent discovered Lamina skills without hints — treatment includes AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md workflow guidance.
Does not claim runtime/test pass rates from post-hoc local analysis — scoring is in-container via Harbor Rewardkit only.
Does not claim model independence from a single agent pin.
Clarify stall rate is diagnostic only — not part of the claim composite.

rejected_alternatives.why_rejected: Harness-forced slash-command phases measure pipeline efficacy when scripted, not SkillsBench-style paired skills evaluation. Removed in v1 Harbor-only migration.
Harbor has no native mid-run Q&A recovery; custom auto-reply reintroduces harness babysitting and muddies the skills claim.
